meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' wall by mastering SaaS pricing models. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to build profitable, high-conversion billing engines fast.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing abstract ideas. The most profitable SaaS projects solve billing complexity. By building specialized pricing engines—tiered, usage-based, or seat-based—you create high-value infrastructure clients will pay for immediately. Use Antigravity to scaffold the logic and Claude Code to deploy the architecture, turning a blank slate into a revenue-generating asset.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the cursor blinking in a rhythmic, mocking taunt. You have the technical stack, the freelance capacity, and the hunger, but the 'what' remains elusive. Most developers mistake 'project ideas' for 'product features,' ignoring the most lucrative layer of any SaaS: the pricing and billing engine. It’s the one feature every client is desperate for but terrified to build from scratch.

We’re shifting the focus from generic CRUD apps to specialized SaaS pricing infrastructure. By mastering the architecture of recurring revenue, you solve your 'no ideas' problem while positioning yourself as a high-ticket consultant. Let’s leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build a robust, modular pricing engine that acts as a plug-and-play solution for your future client work.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Schema Architecture
steps.description: Identify the data models needed for tiered and usage-based billing. We need a flexible schema that handles plan upgrades, mid-cycle changes, and prorated calculations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS pricing system. Include tables for 'Plans', 'Features', 'Subscriptions', and 'UsageRecords'. Ensure relationships support plan upgrades and downgrades.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --tasks 'Generate prisma/schema.prisma with plans and subscription models'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Webhook Handlers
steps.description: The backbone of any pricing engine is the synchronization between Stripe and your local database. We need idempotent webhook handlers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Next.js API route for Stripe webhooks. Handle 'customer.subscription.updated' and 'invoice.payment_succeeded' events to sync database state with Stripe state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --tasks 'Create app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts with secure signature verification'

steps.title: Build Dynamic Pricing UI Components
steps.description: Create a high-converting pricing table that reacts to user state, highlighting the 'Recommended' tier based on feature flags.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive React pricing table component using Tailwind CSS. Include logic to toggle between monthly and yearly billing cycles with dynamic price calculation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --tasks 'Generate components/PricingTable.tsx with annual/monthly toggle logic'

steps.title: Add Usage-Based Metering Logic
steps.description: Implement a service layer that checks usage limits before allowing restricted actions, preventing feature abuse.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript service class 'UsageGuard' that checks if a user has remaining credits for a specific feature before allowing execution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --tasks 'Create lib/usage-guard.ts with checkLimit(userId, featureKey) method'

steps.title: Final Integration and Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a full system integration test, simulating a user upgrade path from Free to Pro.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of Playwright tests to verify the full user flow: signup -> view pricing -> checkout -> subscription active state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --tasks 'Run e2e test suite for Stripe checkout flow'

pro_tips: Always store a 'price_id' from Stripe in your database; never hardcode prices in your frontend.
Implement 'grace periods' in your database model to prevent immediate service disruption during payment failures.
Use metadata fields in Stripe to map your internal 'feature_flags' directly to subscription plans.
Always build a 'hidden' admin dashboard to manually override subscription statuses for client support.
Use event-driven architecture for billing; never block the main thread waiting for a Stripe API response.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing engine to a staging environment, connect it to your Stripe test mode keys, and document the API endpoints so you can reuse this module across all future client SaaS projects.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade pricing engine that solves a universal SaaS pain point. This is the difference between a coder and a high-value architect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
